text:
The whole scene is amazing.
1. He doesn't pay to use the payphone
2. He doesn't type the number, he literally just puts his hand over the entire keypad and it works
3. He starts speaking immediately without hearing if his mum answered, or if it was even his mum answering at all
4. No dial-tone, it immediately gets through to her

I love it

author: @ACuriousBookworm

text:
YoungAuthorAdam
Are there?  My mistake.  I should have refreshed my memory before opening my big mouth.  Also, it was created by the people who made The Simpsons a success.  Bill Oakley and...  Refresh my memory.  Who was the other one?
author: @TheCeejReturns

text: It's no problem.  Josh Weinstein is the other person you're thinking of.
author: @ACuriousBookworm

text:
YoungAuthorAdam
Yes, that's him.  And both of them were on the ground floor of the Simpsons, as writers, when creator Matt Groening was the head writer, where they worked until they left to create Mission Hill...  And that's about when The Simpsons started going downhill, unfortunately.
